---
title: "Taxonomic assignment rate"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
# --- 2.1 Load Processed Data ---

# Load the necessary libraries
library(tidyverse)

# 2.1.1 Load the cleaned data objects saved from the previous script
illumina_processed <- readRDS(file = "processed_data/illumina_processed.rds")
nanopore_processed <- readRDS(file = "processed_data/nanopore_processed.rds")

print("Cleaned data loaded successfully.")

# --- 2.1.2 Data Transformation for Readability ---
# Create a "Genus species" format in the species column, with an exception for TM7 taxa.

# --- For Illumina data (no change) ---
illumina_processed <- illumina_processed %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    species = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(genus) & !is.na(species),
      stringr::str_c(genus, species, sep = " "),
      species
    )
  )

# --- For Nanopore data (with TM7 exception) ---
nanopore_processed <- nanopore_processed %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    species = dplyr::if_else(
      # Condition to combine:
      # 1. Both genus and species are available (not NA)
      # 2. AND the species column does NOT contain the string "TM7"
      !is.na(genus) & !is.na(species) & !stringr::str_starts(species, "TM7"),
      
      # If TRUE, combine them with a space
      stringr::str_c(genus, species, sep = " "),
      
      # If FALSE (if it's TM7 or if species/genus is NA), keep the original species value
      species 
    )
  )

# --- Verify the change ---
print("--- Head of Illumina data with updated species format ---")
illumina_processed %>% dplyr::select(genus, species) %>% head()

print("--- Head of Nanopore data with updated species format ---")
nanopore_processed %>% dplyr::select(genus, species) %>% head()

# You can also specifically check a TM7-related taxon to confirm it was NOT changed
print("--- Verification of TM7 exception ---")
nanopore_processed %>% 
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(species, "TM7")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(genus, species)
```

```{r}
# ---- 2.2 Compare taxonomic assignment rates ----
# 2.2.1 Define calculation function
#' Calculate Taxonomic Assignment Rates
#'
#' This function takes a processed abundance table and calculates the assignment rate
#' (i.e., the percentage of total abundance that is not NA) for each taxonomic level.
#'
#' @param processed_df A processed data frame (e.g., illumina_final or nanopore_final).
#' @param sequencer_name A string to label the data source (e.g., "Illumina").
#' @return A data frame with assignment rates for each sample and taxonomic level.

calculate_assignment_rates <- function(processed_df, sequencer_name) {
  
  taxa_levels <- c("kingdom", "phylum", "class", "order", "family", "genus", "species")
  
  # Convert to long format
  long_data <- processed_df %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = where(is.numeric),
      names_to = "Sample",
      values_to = "Abundance"
    )

  # Calculate assignment rates for each level
  assignment_rates <- purrr::map_dfr(taxa_levels, function(level) {
    long_data %>%
      dplyr::group_by(Sample) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(
        Assignment_Rate_Percent = sum(Abundance[!is.na(!!rlang::sym(level))]),
        .groups = 'drop'
      ) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(Taxonomic_Level = level)
  }) %>%
    # Add a column to identify the sequencer
    dplyr::mutate(Sequencer = sequencer_name)
  
  return(assignment_rates)
}
```

```{r}
# 2.2.2 Apply function and combine
# Calculate rates for Illumina data
illumina_rates <- calculate_assignment_rates(illumina_processed, "Illumina")

# Calculate rates for Nanopore data
nanopore_rates <- calculate_assignment_rates(nanopore_processed, "ONT")

# Combine the two results into a single data frame
combined_rates <- dplyr::bind_rows(illumina_rates, nanopore_rates)

# --- View the combined results for each sample ---
print("--- Combined assignment rates for each sample ---")
print(combined_rates)
```
```{r}
# 2.2.3 Summarize for plotting

# Define the order for the plot
taxa_levels <- c("kingdom", "phylum", "class", "order", "family", "genus", "species")

# Calculate mean and SD, grouping by both Taxonomic_Level and Sequencer
final_summary_table <- combined_rates %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Sequencer, Taxonomic_Level) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Mean_Assignment_Rate = mean(Assignment_Rate_Percent),
    SD_Assignment_Rate = sd(Assignment_Rate_Percent),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  # Use factor to ensure the correct order in the plot
  dplyr::mutate(Taxonomic_Level = factor(Taxonomic_Level, levels = taxa_levels)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Taxonomic_Level)

# --- View the final summary table ---
print("--- Final summary table with mean and SD by sequencer ---")
print(final_summary_table)
```
```{r}
# 2.2.4 Visualize the comparison (B&W with Individual Data Points) 

# This plot uses TWO data frames:
# - final_summary_table: for the summary bars and error bars
# - combined_rates: for the individual data points

assignment_rate_plot <- final_summary_table %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Taxonomic_Level, y = Mean_Assignment_Rate, fill = Sequencer)) +
  
  # 1. Bar chart for the mean (semi-transparent)
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(), color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # 2. Error bars for standard deviation
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = Mean_Assignment_Rate - SD_Assignment_Rate, ymax = Mean_Assignment_Rate + SD_Assignment_Rate),
    width = 0.25,
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +

 # --- 3. Jittered points (NEW, CORRECTED VERSION) ---
  geom_jitter(
    data = combined_rates,
    # Use 'color = Sequencer' to provide the grouping for position_jitterdodge
    aes(y = Assignment_Rate_Percent, color = Sequencer), 
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.9),
    size = 2.0,
    shape = 21,
    fill = "white" # The fill remains white
    # We remove the static 'color = "black"' here because it's now mapped in aes()
  ) +
  # Add a manual color scale to force the outline of BOTH groups to be black
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Illumina" = "black", "ONT" = "black"),
    # Hide this redundant legend
    guide = "none" 
 ) +  

  # (The rest of the theme and labels are the same)
  scale_fill_grey(start = 0.5, end = 0.8) +
  labs(
    # title = "Comparison of Taxonomic Assignment Rates",
    # subtitle = "Illumina vs. ONT",
    x = "Taxonomic level",
    y = "Assignment rate (%)" # Renamed for clarity
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/fig3_assignment_rate_comparison.png", # File path and name
  plot = assignment_rate_plot,                                # The plot object to save
  width = 7,                                                 # Width of the plot in inches
  height = 5,                                                # Height of the plot in inches
  dpi = 300                                                  # Resolution for publication quality
)

# Finally, display the plot in your R Markdown output
print(assignment_rate_plot)
```
